name: an alias map ties a tool reporting id up to a CTP rule so its findings bubble up under that CTP rule
command: printf '%s' '{"version":"2.1.0","runs":[{"tool":{"driver":{"name":"checkov"}},"results":[{"ruleId":"CKV_K8S_16","level":"error","message":{"text":"x"}},{"ruleId":"CKV_K8S_16","level":"error","message":{"text":"y"}}]}]}' > a.sarif && printf '%s' '{"checkov/CKV_K8S_16":"g-k8s-no-privileged-container"}' > al.json && bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/rubric/group-findings-by-rule.sh" --dir . --alias al.json --json 2>/dev/null | node -e 'const j=JSON.parse(require("fs").readFileSync(0,"utf8"));const g=j.rules["g-k8s-no-privileged-container"];process.exit(g&&g.count===2&&g.reporting_ids.includes("checkov/CKV_K8S_16")?0:1)' && echo alias-rolls-up-to-ctp-rule 1>&2
